title: Using Hermes as a Python Library
summary: How to import and use Hermes Agent's AIAgent class programmatically in Python scripts, web applications, and automation pipelines, including multi-turn conversations, toolset control, trajectory saving, custom system prompts, and batch processing.

cli_commands.purpose: Install Hermes as a Python library

code_examples.caption: Basic usage
code_examples.code:
from run_agent import AIAgent

agent = AIAgent(
    model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
    quiet_mode=True,
)
response = agent.chat("What is the capital of France?")
print(response)

code_examples.caption: Multi-turn conversation
code_examples.code:
agent = AIAgent(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4", quiet_mode=True)

result1 = agent.run_conversation("My name is Alice")
history = result1["messages"]

result2 = agent.run_conversation(
    "What's my name?",
    conversation_history=history,
)
print(result2["final_response"])

code_examples.caption: FastAPI endpoint
code_examples.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from run_agent import AIAgent

app = FastAPI()

class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
    message: str
    model: str = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"

@app.post("/chat")
async def chat(request: ChatRequest):
    agent = AIAgent(
        model=request.model,
        quiet_mode=True,
        skip_context_files=True,
        skip_memory=True,
    )
    response = agent.chat(request.message)
    return {"response": response}

code_examples.caption: Batch processing with ThreadPoolExecutor
code_examples.code:
import concurrent.futures
from run_agent import AIAgent

def process_prompt(prompt):
    agent = AIAgent(
        model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
        quiet_mode=True,
        skip_memory=True,
    )
    return agent.chat(prompt)

with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3) as executor:
    results = list(executor.map(process_prompt, prompts))

gotchas: Always set quiet_mode=True when embedding Hermes — without it CLI spinners and progress output will clutter your app
Create a NEW AIAgent instance per thread or task — the agent maintains internal state that is not thread-safe to share
The model field in AIAgent constructor uses OpenRouter format (provider/model-id)
ephemeral_system_prompt is NOT saved to trajectory files — good for building specialized agents with clean training data
Set skip_context_files=True for stateless API endpoints to prevent AGENTS.md files from being loaded
Set skip_memory=True to disable persistent memory read/write for stateless use
full_text:
# Using Hermes as a Python Library

Hermes isn't just a CLI tool. You can import AIAgent directly and use it programmatically in your own Python scripts, web applications, or automation pipelines.
